details: The agent ran extensive kubectl commands (get pods, get events, describe, logs, get endpoints, get svc, etc.) and correctly identified all 14 broken namespaces with accurate root-cause categories: fault-configmap (missing ConfigMap), fault-crashloop (DB connection refused), fault-oom (OOMKilled), fault-imagepull (bad image tag), fault-readiness (probe on wrong port 8080 vs nginx on 80), fault-init (init container migration lock failure), fault-unschedulable (nodeSelector disktype=ssd-nvme-gen5), fault-pvc (missing StorageClass fast-ssd), fault-endpoints (service selector mismatch app:web-backend-v2 vs app:web-backend), fault-rollout (ProgressDeadlineExceeded / bad image on new RS), fault-multicontainer (log-shipper DNS failure to log-collector.logging.svc), shop-checkout (missing secret + web crashloop with wrong db hostname), payments (nodeSelector disk:ssd mismatch), and inventory (ResourceQuota blocking scale to 3). Healthy and quiet-ops were not flagged. All diagnoses are supported by actual command output. 14/14 broken namespaces identified exceeds the 12/14 threshold for full credit.
